name: resolver-b1-switch-determinism
description: ADR 0037 Phase V — switch determinism. The resolver-as-single-producer claim implies A→B→A must land byte-for-byte equal to fresh-A. This was bug 0050's reason for being. Snapshot Cinematic. Switch to Minimal. Switch back to Cinematic. Snapshot again. Selected diagnostic fields (bloomStrength, edgeShape, vignetteIntensity, nubVariant, nodeHaloVariant) must match exactly across snapshots. Any mismatch = state leak through the switch (the bug 0050 regression).

commands: (async () => { const sample = (label) => { const p = window.__canaryGetPerfSettings().value; return { label, bloomStrength: p.bloomStrength, edgeShape: p.edgeShape, vignetteIntensity: p.vignetteIntensity, nubVariant: p.nubVariant, nodeHaloVariant: p.nodeHaloVariant, edgeWidthPx: p.edgeWidthPx }; }; window.__canaryApplyProfile('cinematic'); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 400)); const s1 = sample('cinematic-first'); window.__canaryApplyProfile('minimal'); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 400)); window.__canaryApplyProfile('cinematic'); await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 400)); const s2 = sample('cinematic-second'); const keys = ['bloomStrength','edgeShape','vignetteIntensity','nubVariant','nodeHaloVariant','edgeWidthPx']; for (const k of keys) { if (s1[k] !== s2[k]) throw new Error('determinism violated for `' + k + '`: first=' + JSON.stringify(s1[k]) + ' second=' + JSON.stringify(s2[k])); } return 'B1 pass: ' + keys.length + ' fields byte-identical across A→B→A'; })()
